commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c421befc8e44df30986cd0a2c0845f2d0709d258)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes OpenClaw's JSON/text atomic write helpers to default parent directory creation to 0700, wraps writeJson, adds dirMode regression tests, and adds an Unreleased changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +53, Docs +6. Total +70 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main writes update-check.json through writeJson, and the dependency-backed atomic writer chmods the parent directory using a umask-derived default. I did not run the end-to-end stat repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer/security approval of the writer-scope decision before any merge path is safe.

target.title: fix(security): cap state directory writes to 0700
target.body:
## Summary

`writeTextAtomic` computes `dirMode` as `0o777 & ~process.umask()`, which yields `0o775` under the common `umask 0o002`. When the atomic replace helper ensures the parent directory exists, it sets that mode, relaxing a previously hardened `~/.openclaw` from `0700` back to `0775`.

The same applies to `writeJson`, which re-exports from `@openclaw/fs-safe/json` without overriding `dirMode`.

This fix:
- Defaults `dirMode` to `0o700` in `writeTextAtomic` (was `0o777 & ~process.umask()`)
- Wraps `writeJson` to also default `dirMode: 0o700`
- Adds 4 regression tests verifying both functions and their override paths

Closes #89589

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** State directory permissions are relaxed from 0700 to 0775 by atomic JSON writes under umask 0002. `openclaw security audit` warns repeatedly.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw built from patched source at `/tmp/wt-89589`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  cd /tmp/wt-89589
  pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  pnpm build

  # Verify the fix is active in the production bundle
  grep -n 'dirMode' dist/io-CC2gAU85.js

  # Run the regression tests
  npx vitest run src/infra/json-files.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build:

  The fix is active in the production bundle (448 = 0o700 decimal):

  ```console
  $ grep -n 'dirMode' dist/io-CC2gAU85.js
  3832:dirMode: 448,
  4490:dirMode: 448,
  5221:dirMode: 448,
  ```

  No remaining `0o777` or `umask()` patterns in the atomic write path:

  ```console
  $ grep -c 'dirMode: 511\|0o777\|umask' dist/io-CC2gAU85.js
  0
  ```

  Terminal confirms all 4 new regression tests exercise both the default and override paths:

  ```console
  $ npx vitest run src/infra/json-files.test.ts --reporter=verbose
  ✓ dirMode defaults (#89589) > writeTextAtomic defaults dirMode to 0o700
  ✓ dirMode defaults (#89589) > writeTextAtomic respects explicit dirMode override
  ✓ dirMode defaults (#89589) > writeJson defaults dirMode to 0o700
  ✓ dirMode defaults (#89589) > writeJson respects explicit dirMode override
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  20 passed (20)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Production bundle at `dist/io-CC2gAU85.js` uses `dirMode: 448` (0o700) for all three atomic write call sites. No residual `0o777 & ~process.umask()` pattern remains. State directory permissions are preserved at the user-hardened mode.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end test with a running gateway writing `update-check.json` under `umask 0002` and verifying `stat` output on the parent directory.
target.author: sebtardif
